sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Now I see:
- id=4 (charcoal L-shape): color=c, pixels=45, boundary=[[15, 0], [15, 8], [17, 8], [17, 3], [18, 2], [23, 2], [23, 0]]
  - This is the dark gray L-shape on the left. It's 45 pixels.
- id=9: W, 1 pixel at [22, 61]
- id=10 (yellow L-shape): color=Y, pixels=38, boundary=[[26, 63], [63, 63]]
  - This is the yellow L-shape in the bottom right. It's 38 pixels.
So the dark gray L-shape has returned to its original size of 45 pixels! It seems like when the black L-shape crosses the divider to the left, the dark gray L-shape reappears on the left side at its original size.
So the two L-shapes are swapping sides! When the black L-shape moves all the way to the left, the dark gray L-shape reappears on the left side.
So the two L-shapes are swapping sides!
So the goal is to completely overlap the black L-shape with the dark gray L-shape.
But RIGHT moves them apart! So I need to press LEFT to move them together!
Wait, but LEFT moves them apart! So I need to press RIGHT to move them together!
Wait, let me think again.
- When I press LEFT, the black L-shape moves left, and the dark gray L-shape moves right. So they move apart!
- When I press RIGHT, the black L-shape moves right, and the dark gray L-shape moves left. So they move together!
So RIGHT moves them together!
So I need to keep pressing RIGHT to move them together!
I'm tracking the L-shapes'
